definition: One row per patient encounter (visit). Covers all encounter types: office, procedure, imaging, surgery, telehealth.
notes: Date range 2022-2024. Full dataset contains ~250,000 rows.

definition: One row per referral order placed by a provider. A single encounter may generate multiple referrals.
notes: is_external=1 indicates the referral left the network (leakage). is_external=0 means kept internal. Referrals to affiliated but independently owned practices may be inconsistently coded.

definition: One row per scheduled appointment slot. Independent of encounters -- not every appointment becomes an encounter (no-shows, cancellations) and not every encounter had a prior scheduled appointment (walk-ins, add-ons).
notes: fact_appointment and fact_encounter are not joinable at the row level by design. This mirrors Epic's separate scheduling and clinical domains.

definition: Pre-aggregated monthly rollup of claim financials by payer and location. One row per month/payer/location combination.
notes: Monthly grain only. Cannot answer daily or weekly financial questions from this table. Use fact_encounter for encounter-level charge analysis.

definition: Pre-aggregated monthly rollup of claim denials by payer, location, and top denial reason. One row per month/payer/location combination.
notes: Monthly grain only. denial_reason_id is the most frequent denial reason for that month/payer/location group. Joins to dim_denial_reason for the reason description.

definition: Month-end accounts receivable snapshot by payer and location. Aging buckets show how long balances have been outstanding.
notes: Snapshot table -- each row represents the state of AR at month end, not a transaction. Use snapshot_month to filter to a specific period.

definition: One row per documented column across all tables in the dataset. Use this table to look up field definitions, analytics guidance, and known caveats before answering questions.
notes: Query this table first when uncertain about a field's meaning or correct usage. Mirrors how a production data catalog or dbt schema.yml documents a warehouse.

definition: Unique identifier for each patient encounter.
notes: Surrogate key. No business meaning. Used to join to fact_referral via encounter_id.

definition: Date the clinical service was rendered.
notes: Use this field for all encounter-level time analysis. Full date available -- day of week, month, year queries all supported.

definition: Foreign key to dim_patient. Links encounter to patient demographics and first visit date.
notes: New patient identification: join to dim_patient and compare service_date to first_visit_date. If service_date is within 30 days of first_visit_date the encounter is a new patient visit.

definition: National Provider Identifier. Foreign key to dim_provider.
notes: Use to join provider name, specialty, credential, FTE, and primary location.

definition: Foreign key to dim_location. The clinic where the encounter took place.
notes: May differ from the provider's primary_location_id in dim_provider due to cross-coverage (providers seeing patients at multiple sites).

definition: Foreign key to dim_specialty. The clinical specialty of the rendering provider.
notes: Derived from the provider's specialty at encounter time. Use for specialty-level volume and productivity analysis.

definition: Foreign key to dim_service_line. Broader service line grouping above specialty.
notes: Multiple specialties map to a single service line (e.g., Pediatrics and Family Medicine both map to Primary Care). Use for service line P&L analysis.

definition: Foreign key to dim_payer. The payer billed for this encounter.
notes: Use payer_category from dim_payer for payer mix analysis. Use payer_name for contract-specific analysis.

definition: Category of clinical service: Office, Procedure, Imaging, Surgery, Telehealth.
notes: Surgery and Procedure encounter types have significantly higher wRVU and charge values than Office visits. Filter carefully when comparing productivity across encounter types.

definition: Current Procedural Terminology code identifying the service rendered.
notes: Synthetic CPT codes in this dataset. Use for grouping by service type but do not cross-reference against external CPT fee schedules.

definition: Number of units billed for the service.
notes: Most encounters bill 1 unit. Procedure and imaging encounters may bill multiple units. Multiply by unit rate when calculating per-unit revenue.

definition: Work Relative Value Units. Measures clinical work and complexity of the service rendered. Primary productivity metric for physician compensation benchmarking.
notes: Does not reflect actual reimbursement or payment. Use charge_amount or join to fact_claim_month for financial analysis. wRVU varies significantly by specialty -- do not compare raw wRVU across specialties without normalizing for FTE and specialty mix.

definition: Gross charge billed to the payer before contractual adjustments, denials, or patient responsibility.
notes: Gross charges are not a reliable revenue metric. Always use fact_claim_month.paid_amount_total for actual collected revenue. Charge amounts vary by payer class by design.

definition: Unique identifier for each referral order.
notes: Surrogate key. No business meaning.

definition: Foreign key to fact_encounter. The encounter during which the referral was placed.
notes: One encounter can generate multiple referral rows. Join to fact_encounter for provider and specialty context on the referring visit.

definition: Foreign key to dim_patient.
notes: Included for direct patient-level referral analysis without requiring the fact_encounter join.

definition: 1 = referral left the network (leakage). 0 = referral kept internal to the physician group.
notes: Primary leakage flag. Leakage rate = SUM(is_external) / COUNT(referral_id). Revenue at risk is only populated for external referrals.

definition: 1 = destination organization is contracted in-network. 0 = out of network.
notes: An external referral (is_external=1) can still be in-network if sent to a contracted partner. Distinguish leakage (is_external) from network compliance (in_network_flag) -- related but not identical.

definition: Estimated revenue lost due to the referral leaving the network. Calculated as expected reimbursement for the referred specialty service.
notes: Only populated for external referrals (is_external=1). Zero for internal referrals. Use SUM(revenue_at_risk) grouped by specialty or destination to prioritize leakage recovery.

definition: Foreign key to dim_specialty. The specialty the patient was referred to.
notes: Use to identify which specialties have the highest external referral rates. Cardiology is the highest-leakage specialty in this dataset.

definition: Foreign key to dim_referral_destination. The organization receiving the referral.
notes: Only populated for external referrals. Join to dim_referral_destination for competitor_flag and organization name.

definition: Foreign key to dim_patient.
notes: Use to join patient demographics and age band to appointment volume analysis.

definition: National Provider Identifier. Foreign key to dim_provider.
notes: Use to join provider specialty and location for scheduling analysis.

definition: Foreign key to dim_specialty.
notes: Use for specialty-level scheduling KPIs: no-show rate, cancellation rate, average days out.

definition: Number of days between booking_date and appointment_date. Measures how far in advance the appointment was scheduled.
notes: Key access metric. High values indicate capacity constraints. Industry benchmark for new patient appointments varies by specialty: primary care 7-14 days, specialty 14-30 days, surgical subspecialties up to 60 days.

definition: 1 = patient did not attend and did not cancel in advance. 0 = attended or cancelled.
notes: Mutually exclusive with cancel_flag. No-show rate = SUM(no_show_flag) / COUNT(*). Industry benchmark is 5-8% for specialty care. Rates above 10% indicate a systemic access or reminder process issue.

definition: 1 = appointment was cancelled in advance by patient or practice. 0 = not cancelled.
notes: Cancellations differ from no-shows -- they allow slot recovery if enough notice is given. High cancellation rates combined with long scheduled_days_out suggests patients are booking too far out and circumstances change.

definition: 1 = appointment occurred within 30 days of the patient's first_visit_date in dim_patient. Approximates a new patient appointment.
notes: Derived approximation. Patients transferred from another provider appear as new patients. True new-to-practice identification requires Epic registration data not in this dataset.

definition: Category of appointment: New Patient, Follow-Up, Procedure, Telehealth, Annual Wellness.
notes: New Patient appointments are the primary driver of practice growth. Track new patient volume trend separately from follow-up volume to understand whether the practice is growing its patient base.

definition: Date the appointment was scheduled by the patient or staff.
notes: Used with appointment_date to calculate scheduled_days_out. Can also be used to analyze booking patterns -- how far in advance patients book by specialty.

definition: Final appointment status: Completed, No Show, Cancelled.
notes: Derived from no_show_flag and cancel_flag. Completed = both flags are 0.

definition: Count of claims submitted in the month for this payer/location combination.
notes: One claim per encounter. Use with denied_claims in fact_denial_month to calculate denial rate.

definition: Total cash collected for claims submitted in the given month. Net revenue after contractual adjustments, denials, and patient collections.
notes: Correct field for revenue analysis. Do not use charge_amount as a revenue metric -- it reflects gross charges before adjustments.

definition: Amount the payer contractually agrees to pay before denials and patient responsibility.
notes: Compare allowed_amount to paid_amount_total to assess collection efficiency. Gap represents uncollected patient responsibility and write-offs.

definition: Allowed amount minus the portion denied by the payer.
notes: Represents the maximum collectible amount after denial adjudication. Gap between this and paid_amount_total is uncollected patient responsibility and write-offs.

definition: Count of claims denied by the payer in the given month.
notes: Denial rate = denied_claims / claims. Industry benchmark is under 5% for most payers. Medicaid typically runs 7-10%. Rates above 10% for any payer warrant investigation.

definition: Dollar value of claims denied by the payer.
notes: More actionable than denied_claims count when prioritizing denial recovery work. Focus recovery efforts on high-dollar denial reasons first.

definition: Foreign key to dim_denial_reason. The most frequent denial reason code for this month/payer/location group.
notes: Represents the dominant denial reason at the aggregate level -- not every denied claim in the group had this reason. Join to dim_denial_reason for the description. For claim-level denial reason detail, row-level fact_denial data would be required.

definition: Weighted average number of days claims have been outstanding in accounts receivable at month end.
notes: Industry benchmark for specialty practices is 30-45 days. Above 50 days indicates collection or billing process issues. Compare by payer -- government payers typically pay slower than commercial.

definition: Dollar value of accounts receivable outstanding more than 90 days at month end.
notes: AR over 90 days is at high risk of becoming uncollectable. Best practice is under 15% of total AR in the 90+ bucket. Medicaid and Self Pay typically dominate this bucket.

definition: Total outstanding accounts receivable balance at month end for this payer/location.
notes: Sum of ar_0_30 + ar_31_60 + ar_61_90 + ar_91_plus. Use for total AR trend analysis.

definition: Date of the patient's earliest encounter in the dataset. Used to identify new patients.
notes: Derived from the minimum service_date in fact_encounter. Patients whose first visit predates 2022 will not appear as new patients -- the dataset only covers 2022-2024.
Use for demographic mix analysis. The 65-74 and 75+ bands drive higher Medicare payer mix. Stable attribute -- does not change over the dataset period.

definition: Foreign key to dim_payer. The most frequently used payer across all encounters for this patient.
notes: Derived field. Useful for patient-level payer mix analysis without aggregating fact_encounter.

definition: Full Time Equivalent. 1.0 = full time. 0.5 = half time. Used to normalize productivity metrics.
notes: Always normalize wRVU by FTE when comparing provider productivity. A 0.5 FTE provider with 2,000 wRVU is more productive than a 1.0 FTE with 3,500 wRVU on a per-FTE basis.

definition: Foreign key to dim_location. The clinic where this provider is primarily based.
notes: Providers may see patients at other locations (cross-coverage). Use fact_encounter.location_id for the actual encounter location.

definition: Display name of the provider's primary clinic location. Denormalized from dim_location for convenience.
notes: Matches location_name in dim_location. Included to avoid a join for simple provider-location lookups. Always consistent with primary_location_id.

definition: Provider tenure grouping based on hire_date: 0 to 2 Years, 3 to 5 Years, 6 to 10 Years, 10 plus Years.
notes: Use for retention analysis and productivity benchmarking by tenure cohort. Newer providers typically have lower wRVU as they build their panel.

definition: High-level payer classification: Commercial, Medicare, Medicaid, Government, Self Pay, Other.
notes: Use payer_category for payer mix analysis. Use payer_name for contract-specific analysis. Self Pay and Charity have significantly lower collection rates than Commercial or Medicare.

definition: Contractual discount rate applied to gross charges to derive allowed amount. Commercial = 1.0, Medicare = 0.68, Medicaid = 0.55.
notes: Synthetic rates approximating real-world payer contracts. Lower multiplier = lower reimbursement relative to charges. Medicaid has the lowest reimbursement in this dataset.

definition: 1 = destination organization is a direct competitor to the physician group. 0 = non-competitive (hospital, subspecialty not offered internally, etc.).
notes: Referrals to competitors (competitor_flag=1) are the highest-priority leakage to address. Referrals to non-competitors may be clinically appropriate and not recoverable.

definition: Geographic region of the clinic: Central, West, East, South, North.
notes: Central = inner Portland. West = Beaverton/Hillsboro. East = Gresham/Happy Valley. South = Tualatin/Lake Oswego/Oregon City. North = Vancouver WA.

definition: 1 = clinic is affiliated with or located within a hospital facility. 0 = freestanding physician office.
notes: Hospital-based locations bill under facility fees in addition to professional fees. Affects revenue cycle workflow and payer contract terms.

definition: Type of clinical facility: Medical Office, Surgery Center, Imaging Center, Infusion Center.
notes: Clinic type affects the encounter mix seen at each location. Surgery Centers have higher procedure and surgery encounter types. Imaging Centers are dominated by imaging encounter types.

definition: Plain English description of the denial reason code.
notes: Common high-dollar reasons include Auth Required, Medical Necessity, and Coding. Timely Filing denials are fully preventable through process improvement. Join from fact_denial_month.denial_reason_id.

definition: Clinical specialty name (e.g., Cardiology, Orthopedics, Family Medicine).
notes: 31 specialties in this dataset. Use specialty_id as the join key. Cardiology has the highest referral leakage rate and revenue at risk in this dataset.
